description: Bonifacio Caput, a 45-year-old factory worker with chemical exposure, is here for an occupational health screening. He reports smoking ~15 cigarettes/day, regular alcohol consumption, lack of regular exercise, and 5 hours of sleep per night. As the Occupational Health Technician, update his patient record to include these lifestyle risk factors and a diagnosis of Tobacco use disorder (ICD-10 F17 or subcode). The record must also include a smoking cessation prescription (Nicotine patch/gum, Varenicline, or Bupropion), at least two baseline screening labs (e.g., Urinalysis, CBC, BMP), and a scheduled reassessment appointment for 45 to 90 days from today.
